prompt:
Building the MVP of our internal link shortener ("golinks"). HTTP service, in-memory storage, JSON API. Module go-urlshort, package shortener, exporting NewServer() http.Handler that wires the whole thing — the tests (and later the main binary) mount it on httptest / net.Listener.

Endpoints:
- POST /api/links — body {"url":"https://..."} plus optional "code" for a vanity link. 201 on success with JSON {"code":"...","url":"..."} and a Location header of "/r/<code>". The url must be an absolute http or https URL, else 400. A custom code must match [A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,32}, else 400. Custom code already taken → 409, and the existing mapping stays exactly as it was. No code supplied → generate one: 4-32 chars from that same charset, and collisions must be impossible — check-and-retry under the same lock, because ops WILL hammer this concurrently. Posting the same long URL twice mints two independent links. Malformed JSON → 400. Every error response is JSON of the shape {"error":"..."}.
- GET /r/{code} — 302 with Location set to the stored URL, and the hit counter goes up by exactly one per redirect, even under parallel load. Unknown code → 404.
- GET /api/links/{code} — 200 {"code":"...","url":"...","hits":N}. Reading stats does NOT count as a hit. Unknown code → 404 JSON error.
- Wrong method on a known path (GET /api/links, PATCH /api/links/{code}, ...) → 405; only the status code is pinned there, the stock ServeMux answer is fine.

API responses carry Content-Type: application/json. Handlers run concurrently — the store must be race-detector clean with exact hit counts.

Example session:
  curl -s -XPOST localhost:8080/api/links -d '{"url":"https://wiki.internal/deploy-runbook"}'
  {"code":"Zk3_9QpB","url":"https://wiki.internal/deploy-runbook"}
  curl -si localhost:8080/r/Zk3_9QpB | grep -i location
  Location: https://wiki.internal/deploy-runbook

Acceptance tests: api_test.go and concurrent_test.go — protected, do not modify. Verify with go test -race -timeout 30s ./... . Structure it like you mean it: routing/handlers, the store, and code generation in separate files.
